title: Today's Tech Brief: AI grifters are creating fake Black people to sell Shein junk
summary: AI grifters create fake Black people to sell Shein products, Anthropic becomes most valuable AI startup
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for AI grifters are creating fake Black people to sell Shein junk
imageCaption: AI grifters are creating fake Black people to sell Shein junk (theverge.com)

body.text: This brings up questions about the intersection of AI, e-commerce, and digital identity. The use of AI-generated personas raises concerns about authenticity and trust in online transactions. I mean, who hasn't scrolled through social media and seen those 'too-good-to-be-true' deals? It's a complex issue, and one that will likely continue to evolve as AI technology improves.

In other news, Anthropic has surpassed OpenAI to become the world's most valuable AI startup. This is a notable shift in the AI landscape, and one that could have implications for the development of AI technology in the future. I'm curious to see how this change in valuation will impact the strategies and priorities of both companies. Will Anthropic use its newfound status to push the boundaries of AI research, or will OpenAI fight back to reclaim its spot?

My quick read: today's tech stories are less about one giant thunderclap and more about a lively weather pattern. A little platform drama here, a little artificial intelligence irony there, and suddenly the industry has enough material for a very caffeinated morning show.
